# Background Music Research - FLUXION Promotional Video
> CoVe 2026 Deep Research | 2026-03-23
> Video: ~4:30 min | Pattern: problem (tension) > solution (relief) > features (energy) > pricing (confidence) > CTA (inspiration)

## 1. BEST FREE ROYALTY-FREE MUSIC SOURCES (2026)

### TIER 1 — FREE, NO ATTRIBUTION, COMMERCIAL OK

| Platform | License | Attribution | Commercial | Download Format | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------------|------------|-----------------|-------|
| **Pixabay Music** | Pixabay Content License | NOT required | YES | MP3 (128kbps free) | BEST OPTION. Irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free. Huge library. No signup needed for browse, account needed for download. |
| **Mixkit** (by Envato) | Mixkit License | NOT required | YES | MP3/WAV | Curated, high quality. No signup needed. Owned by Envato. |
| **Fesliyan Studios** | Custom free license | NOT required (most tracks) | YES | MP3/WAV | Good corporate/cinematic section. Check per-track license. |
| **No-Copyright-Music.com** | CC0 / Custom | NOT required | YES | MP3 | Smaller library but all truly free. |

### TIER 2 — FREE WITH ATTRIBUTION

| Platform | License | Attribution | Commercial | Download Format | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------------|------------|-----------------|-------|
| **Chosic** | CC-BY (most tracks) | Required (credit artist) | YES | MP3 | Excellent curation. Attribution = mention in video description. |
| **Free Stock Music** | CC-BY / CC-BY-NC | Required | Check per track | MP3 | Good piano/cinematic section. CAREFUL: some tracks are CC-BY-NC (no commercial). |
| **Uppbeat** | Uppbeat Free License | Required (Uppbeat credit) | YES | MP3/WAV | 3 free downloads/month on free tier. High quality. Attribution in description. |
| **Bensound** | Bensound License | Required (free tier) | YES | MP3 | High quality. Free tier = attribution required. Paid = no attribution. |
| **YouTube Audio Library** | YouTube License | Some require, some don't | YES (YouTube only) | MP3 | Only accessible via YouTube Studio. Designed for YouTube videos specifically. |

### TIER 3 — FREE TRIAL / FREEMIUM (NOT RECOMMENDED)

| Platform | Notes |
|----------|-------|
| **Artlist** | NO free tier. 14-day trial but downloads watermarked. $9.99/mo minimum. SKIP. |
| **Epidemic Sound** | 30-day free trial, but tracks get flagged after trial ends. SKIP. |
| **Storyblocks** | Free trial available but similar issues. SKIP. |

### VERDICT: USE PIXABAY MUSIC
- Zero attribution, zero cost, commercial use OK
- Largest free library of cinematic/emotional tracks
- Direct MP3 download (create free account)
- No copyright claims on YouTube
- Pixabay Content License is irrevocable and worldwide

## 2. SPECIFIC TRACK RECOMMENDATIONS BY VIDEO SECTION

### STRATEGY A: ONE CONTINUOUS TRACK (~4:30) — PREFERRED

Finding a single track that builds from tension to triumph in exactly 4:30 is ideal. These Pixabay tracks are the best candidates:

#### TOP PICK: "Inspiring Uplifting Motivational Cinematic" (ID: 153862)
- **URL**: https://pixabay.com/music/main-title-inspiring-uplifting-motivational-cinematic-153862/
- **Artist**: White_Records
- **Description**: Begins with beautiful soft piano, then strings join with full orchestra + big drums and percussion
- **Why**: Perfect tension-to-triumph arc. Piano intro (problem) -> strings build (solution) -> full orchestra (features) -> crescendo (CTA)
- **License**: Pixabay Content License (free, no attribution, commercial OK)

## 4. VOLUME MIXING BEST PRACTICES (VOICEOVER + MUSIC)

### STANDARD dB LEVELS

| Element | Peak Level | Average (LUFS) | Notes |
|---------|-----------|----------------|-------|
| **Voiceover narration** | -6 dB to -3 dB peak | -16 LUFS | Primary audio — must be crystal clear |
| **Background music (under voice)** | -24 dB to -18 dB peak | -30 to -35 LUFS | 15-20 dB BELOW voice |
| **Background music (no voice, transitions)** | -12 dB to -6 dB peak | -20 LUFS | Can come up during visual-only moments |
| **Overall mix** | -1 dB true peak max | -14 to -16 LUFS | YouTube loudness target: -14 LUFS |

### KEY RULES

1. **The 20dB Rule**: Background music should be 18-20 dB lower than voiceover. This is the broadcast standard.

2. **Ducking**: Use volume automation (ducking) to lower music when voice starts, raise it during pauses.
   - Voice starts: music drops to -25dB over 0.5s
   - Voice pauses (>1.5s): music rises to -15dB over 0.8s
   - Transitions between sections: music at full volume for 2-3s

3. **EQ Separation**: Cut 2-4kHz from music (the vocal presence range) so voice sits on top cleanly. Boost 2-4kHz slightly on voiceover.

4. **Frequency Selection**: Choose music that avoids the 300Hz-3kHz vocal range. Piano + strings are ideal because they have clear separation from human voice fundamentals.

5. **Test on Multiple Devices**: Check mix on:
   - Studio headphones
   - Phone speaker (most common playback!)
   - Laptop speakers
   - Car speakers

### ffmpeg VOLUME COMMANDS

```bash
# Set music to -20dB below voice
# Voice at 0dB, music at -20dB
ffmpeg -i voice.wav -i music.mp3 \
  -filter_complex "[1:a]volume=0.1[music];[0:a][music]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest" \
  -ac 2 output.mp3

# More precise: voice normalized + music ducked
ffmpeg -i voice.wav -i music.mp3 \
  -filter_complex "[0:a]loudnorm=I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11[voice];[1:a]volume=0.08[music];[voice][music]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest[out]" \
  -map "[out]" output.mp3

# Sidechain compression (auto-ducking) with ffmpeg
ffmpeg -i voice.wav -i music.mp3 \
  -filter_complex "[1:a]asplit[music1][music2];[music1]sidechaincompress=level_in=1:threshold=0.02:ratio=6:attack=200:release=1000[ducked];[0:a][ducked]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest" \
  output.mp3
```

### Audacity Workflow (Manual, More Control)
1. Import voiceover track
2. Import music track
3. Select music track > Effect > Auto Duck
   - Duck amount: -18 dB
   - Inner fade up: 0.5s
   - Outer fade down: 0.5s
4. Export as WAV 48kHz 24-bit (master), then convert to MP3 320kbps for delivery

---

## 5. RECOMMENDED WORKFLOW FOR FLUXION VIDEO

### Step 1: Download & Audition
1. Go to https://pixabay.com/music/ (create free account if needed)
2. Download these 5 tracks (all free, no attribution):
   - **153862** — Inspiring Uplifting Motivational Cinematic (TOP PICK for single track)
   - **156711** — Emotional Piano and Cinematic Strings
   - **155205** — Cinematic Emotional Background Piano & Strings
   - **115915** — Emotional Cinematic Background Piano (build-up category)
   - **139926** — Italian Coves (Mediterranean accent)

### Step 2: Choose Strategy
- **If one track fits the 4:30 arc** -> use it as-is, trim/loop if needed
- **If no single track works** -> pick 2-3 from the section recommendations and crossfade

### Step 3: Mix with Edge-TTS Voiceover
```bash
# Assuming voiceover already generated via Edge-TTS
# Music at 10% volume (approx -20dB)
ffmpeg -i voiceover.wav -i background_music.mp3 \
  -filter_complex "[1:a]volume=0.10,afade=t=in:st=0:d=3,afade=t=out:st=255:d=5[music];[0:a][music]amix=inputs=2:duration=first:dropout_transition=3[out]" \
  -map "[out]" -ac 2 -ar 48000 final_audio.wav
```

### Step 4: Verify
- Play on phone speaker (most PMI owners will watch on phone)
- Voice must be 100% intelligible
- Music should be felt, not heard over voice
- Music should create emotion during visual-only transitions

## FINAL RECOMMENDATION

**Go with Pixabay Music. Download the top 5 tracks listed above. Audition them against the video timeline. The single-track approach with "Inspiring Uplifting Motivational Cinematic" (153862) is the cleanest solution if its duration and arc match your 4:30 video.**

If you need a Mediterranean touch, layer "Italian Coves" (139926) very quietly (-25dB) under the main track during the features section (1:30-3:30) for warmth without clashing.

Mix voice at -16 LUFS, music at -35 LUFS under voice, -20 LUFS during transitions. Test on phone speakers.
